questions.text: A moss exhibits alternation of generations. Which is the dominant phase?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bryophytes have dominant gametophyte phases, with sporophytes dependent on gametophytes for nutrition

questions.explanation: Bryophytes have dominant gametophyte phases, with sporophytes dependent on gametophytes for nutrition

questions.text: In a shaded forest floor, a moss grows successfully despite low water availability. Which adaptation helps it survive?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cuticle reduces water loss, and rhizoids anchor the moss while aiding absorption.

questions.explanation: The cuticle reduces water loss, and rhizoids anchor the moss while aiding absorption.

questions.text: Which of the following groups of plants is identified as tracheophytes due to the presence of vascular tissues?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lycopsida (club mosses) are tracheophytes because they possess vascular tissues (xylem and phloem) for efficient transport of water, minerals, and food. In contrast, Hepaticopsida (liverworts), Bryopsida (mosses), and Anthocerotopsida (hornworts) are non-vascular bryophytes.

questions.explanation: Lycopsida (club mosses) are tracheophytes because they possess vascular tissues (xylem and phloem) for efficient transport of water, minerals, and food. In contrast, Hepaticopsida (liverworts), Bryopsida (mosses), and Anthocerotopsida (hornworts) are non-vascular bryophytes.

questions.text: In flowering plants, four megaspores are produced within the megasporangium, but only one of the megaspores is developed into a healthy female gametophyte, while the remaining three are aborted. The MAIN reason to adopt this strategy by the plants is to
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Increase the chances of variations in the four megaspores
Ensure the maximum protection for the developing megaspores
Decrease the competition for space and food among the four megaspores
options.explanation: By allowing only one megaspore to develop, the plant reduces competition for space and nutrients among the four megaspores. This ensures that the surviving megaspore can grow into a healthy female gametophyte.

options.text: Make the gametophyte suitable to catch maximum number of pollen grains

questions.explanation: By allowing only one megaspore to develop, the plant reduces competition for space and nutrients among the four megaspores. This ensures that the surviving megaspore can grow into a healthy female gametophyte.

questions.text: The advanced characteristics of the sporophyte of hornworts (anthocerotae) that enable them to thrive better on land are the presence of
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Rhizoids which help to anchor the plant
Stomata and chloroplasts in the epidermis
options.explanation: Hornwort sporophytes have stomata for gas exchange and chloroplasts in their epidermal cells, which enhance photosynthesis and adaptation to terrestrial environments. These features help them survive better on land.

options.text: Horn-like projections out of the gametophyte
Mature sporangium that splits open at the ends

questions.explanation: Hornwort sporophytes have stomata for gas exchange and chloroplasts in their epidermal cells, which enhance photosynthesis and adaptation to terrestrial environments. These features help them survive better on land.

questions.text: The cells of the embryo sac of an apple plant include
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Antipodal cells, polar nuclei, sperm cells, and tube cells
Synergids, egg cell, polar nuclei, and antipodal cells
options.explanation: The embryo sac of an apple plant (a typical angiosperm) contains synergids, an egg cell, polar nuclei, and antipodal cells. These cells participate in fertilization and formation of the endosperm.

options.text: Synergids, egg cell and generative cells

questions.explanation: The embryo sac of an apple plant (a typical angiosperm) contains synergids, an egg cell, polar nuclei, and antipodal cells. These cells participate in fertilization and formation of the endosperm.

questions.text: One of the changes that would occur in an ovary of flowering plants after double fertilization is that the
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Antipodal cells will double in number
Polar nuclei will form triploid endosperm
options.explanation: During double fertilization, one sperm fuses with the egg cell to form the zygote, and the other sperm fuses with the polar nuclei to produce triploid endosperm, which nourishes the developing embryo.The pollen tube has already disappeared by the time double fertilization is completed, antipodal cells typically degenerate, and the egg cell fuses with a sperm cell, not a synergid.

options.text: Egg cell will fuse with synergids to form diploid zygote

questions.explanation: During double fertilization, one sperm fuses with the egg cell to form the zygote, and the other sperm fuses with the polar nuclei to produce triploid endosperm, which nourishes the developing embryo.The pollen tube has already disappeared by the time double fertilization is completed, antipodal cells typically degenerate, and the egg cell fuses with a sperm cell, not a synergid.

questions.text: The significance of heterospory in plants is that it
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Heterospory produces two different spore types (microspores & megaspores), which is the key evolutionary step leading to seed formation. This allows development of protected female gametophytes inside the megaspore, enabling seeds to evolve.

options.text: Ensures the development of motile sperm
Provides an efficient way of spore dispersion
Helps in the production of independent gametophytes

questions.explanation: Heterospory produces two different spore types (microspores & megaspores), which is the key evolutionary step leading to seed formation. This allows development of protected female gametophytes inside the megaspore, enabling seeds to evolve.

questions.text: The given diagram shows the life cycle of a plant.The feature in the given life cycle due to which this plant is classified as a tracheophyte is the presence of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The dominance of the sporophyte is a key trait that distinguishes tracheophytes from non-vascular plants like bryophytes, where the gametophyte is dominant.

questions.explanation: The dominance of the sporophyte is a key trait that distinguishes tracheophytes from non-vascular plants like bryophytes, where the gametophyte is dominant.

questions.text: The structure of a plant that characterises a monocot plant is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monocots have flower parts in multiples of 3, while dicots have them in multiples of 4 or 5

questions.explanation: Monocots have flower parts in multiples of 3, while dicots have them in multiples of 4 or 5

questions.text: The given characteristics are present in a sporophyte.•Waxy cuticle •Meristematic tissue Stomata and chloroplast•The subdivision of bryophytes that exhibits the given sporophytic characteristics is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hornworts possess the most complex sporophytes among bryophytes, uniquely featuring a persistent apical meristem, a waxy cuticle, and functional stomata with chloroplasts, which allows the sporophyte to be partially independent.

questions.explanation: Hornworts possess the most complex sporophytes among bryophytes, uniquely featuring a persistent apical meristem, a waxy cuticle, and functional stomata with chloroplasts, which allows the sporophyte to be partially independent.

questions.text: The option that CORRECTLY represents the characteristics of the sporophyte and gametophyte generations of a gymnosperm is
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Independent and dominant, less conspicuous and dependent
options.explanation: In gymnosperms, the life cycle includes two distinct generations: the sporophyte and the gametophyte. The sporophyte generation is the most visible and dominant phase, characterized by being independent and capable of producing spores. In contrast, the gametophyte generation is less conspicuous and dependent on the sporophyte for nutrition and support.

options.text: Less conspicuous and dependent, independent and dominant
Dependent and dominant, more conspicuous and independent
Core conspicuous and independent, dependent and dominant

questions.explanation: In gymnosperms, the life cycle includes two distinct generations: the sporophyte and the gametophyte. The sporophyte generation is the most visible and dominant phase, characterized by being independent and capable of producing spores. In contrast, the gametophyte generation is less conspicuous and dependent on the sporophyte for nutrition and support.

questions.text: The given diagram shows a Marchantia thallus (liverwort).The function of structure X is to
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The gemmae (structure X) produced inside are washed out by rain and grow into new, genetically identical thalli, providing an efficient method of asexual reproduction.

questions.explanation: The gemmae (structure X) produced inside are washed out by rain and grow into new, genetically identical thalli, providing an efficient method of asexual reproduction.

questions.text: In the given diagram of hornwort, antheridia and archegonia are found embedded in which labelled part?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The antheridia and archegonia (gamete-producing structures) are found embedded in the C part, which represents the haploid gametophyte thallus of the hornwort. The gametangia develop within this vegetative tissue to produce the gametes.

questions.explanation: The antheridia and archegonia (gamete-producing structures) are found embedded in the C part, which represents the haploid gametophyte thallus of the hornwort. The gametangia develop within this vegetative tissue to produce the gametes.

questions.text: In club mosses (lycopsida), microphylls are evolved from
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A short stem bearing side branches
Small projecting extensions of stem tissue
options.explanation: Microphylls are small leaves with a single, unbranched vein and are thought to have evolved as simple enations (small, non-vascularized projections) from the stem surface that later gained a single strand of vascular tissue.

options.text: Side branches dividing in the same spatial plane
Infilling of simple tissues between lateral branches

questions.explanation: Microphylls are small leaves with a single, unbranched vein and are thought to have evolved as simple enations (small, non-vascularized projections) from the stem surface that later gained a single strand of vascular tissue.
